text:
There once was an island inhabited entirely by talking cats. They had no need for anything else—they had each other, the tastiest mice they could catch, and a great collection of tales whispered into their ears every night.

Among these feline conversationalists was Mittens, a grand old cat who never dared venture out of his hammock beyond the comfort of tall grasses.

One autumn the leaves turned a fiery orange, and Mittens heard gossip that a curious traveler had come to the island. Not curious enough to leave his hammock, but curious enough to listen.

The traveler was seeking the legendary Nine Lives Pond. It wasn't just any ordinary pond, but it was said to have nine miraculous lives in each drop.

Mittens whispered to his friends who were quite intrigued. They plotted a little adventure.

And so it was set. The first leap was small. They reached the pond's edge.

Then bigger leaps.

Then a splash.

And then nine lives! All cascading into
